title: Reaper (Executor-class)

The '''''Reaper''''' was an [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|''Executor''-class Star Dreadnought]] in service to the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] and a number of its successor states. Once the [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]] of [[Scourge Squadron]] under [[Grand Moff/Legends|Grand Moff]] [[Ardus Kaine]], the ''Reaper'' became the centerpiece of Kaine's effort to continue Imperial rule after the [[Death/Legends|death]] of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] at the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]]. Kaine saw the formation of a new alliance, the [[Pentastar Alignment]], aboard the ''Reaper'', and the [[Starship/Legends|ship]] became a major part of the Alignment's [[Navy/Legends|naval]] power.

Kaine, however, died in [[Operation Shadow Hand]] serving the [[Cloning/Legends|reborn]] Palpatine, and the Alignment and ''Reaper'' were taken in by [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Gilad Pellaeon/Legends|Gilad Pellaeon]] of the [[Imperial Remnant]]. Pellaeon made the ''Reaper'' his own personal [[Commanding officer/Legends|command]] and, with it, conducted a [[Orinda campaign|campaign]] against the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], culminating in a major victory against [[General/Legends|General]] [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]], the ''Executor''-class Star Dreadnought ''[[Lusankya/Legends|Lusankya]]'', and [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] at the [[Battle of Orinda]]. Within a [[Standard year/Legends|year]], however, Pellaeon and the ''Reaper'' were bogged down in a [[Orbital bombardment/Legends|bombardment]] of the New Republic [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Adumar/Legends|Adumar]] when the New Republic invaded the heart of Imperial [[Realspace/Legends|space]]. Pellaeon moved against them, and though he was able to hold the New Republic forces back, the ''Reaper'' was destroyed at the [[Battle of Celanon]].

==Description==
The ''Reaper'' was the third [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|''Executor''-class Star Dreadnought]] manufactured for the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], behind the sister [[Starship/Legends|ships]] [[Executor/Legends|''Executor'']] and [[Lusankya/Legends|''Lusankya'']], the latter of which was constructed in utter secrecy,<ref name="War" /><ref name="SotG">[[Starships of the Galaxy (Saga Edition)|''Starships of the Galaxy'', Saga Edition]]</ref> and the first [[Warship/Legends|warship]] of that class built at [[Kuat Drive Yards/Legends|Kuat Drive Yards]]. It had a [[Bridge (starship)/Legends|bridge]] and several [[Engine/Legends|engines]].<ref name="War" /> A colossal vessel, it saw repeated use as a central part of Imperial [[Fleet/Legends|fleets]], as a [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]] and [[Command ship/Legends|command vessel]]. The securing of the ''Reaper'' was a major coup for Imperial successor states,<ref name="The Essential Chronology" /> and the [[Laser/Legends|firepower]] it provided gave any who [[Commanding officer/Legends|commanded]] it muscle to back up their [[Politics/Legends|political]] power.<ref name="TEA" />

==History==
===Changing hands===
[[File:Pentastar_Alignment.svg|thumb|right|250px|The Pentastar Alignment (emblem pictured) was formed aboard the ''Reaper''.]]
During the reign of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]], the ''Reaper'' was the flagship of [[Scourge Squadron]], an elite [[Task force/Legends|task force]] of [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyers]] assigned to locate and eliminate [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] activities in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="TPA" /> In [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]],<ref name="The Essential Chronology" /> after the [[Death/Legends|death]] of [[Grand Moff/Legends|Grand Moff]] [[Wilhuff Tarkin/Legends|Wilhuff Tarkin]] in the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], his replacement, [[Ardus Kaine]], was given personal command of Scourge Squadron to take the fight to the Rebels and repair the damage caused by the Empire's defeat at [[Yavin Prime/Legends|Yavin]]. Kaine was aboard the ''Reaper'' when news came that the Emperor had been killed in the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]]<ref name="TPA" /> of [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]].<ref name="The New Essential Chronology">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> In the wake of this defeat, Kaine met with four other representatives of major Imperial interests—[[Governor/Legends|Governor]] [[Ib Dekeet]] and [[Commerce Master Commissioner]] [[Gregor Raquoran]] from the Imperial government, and two corporate representatives, [[Wyrn Otro]] of [[Galentro Heavy Works]] and [[Elta Besk]], head of [[Dynamic Automata]]—aboard the ''Reaper'', in what Kaine dubbed the [[Pentastar Talks]]. For [[Standard day/Legends|days]], the group negotiated and bargained with each other aboard the vessel, eventually forging an alliance in the signing of the [[Pentastar Alignment Treaty]], the founding document of the new [[Pentastar Alignment]]<ref name="TPA" /> in 4 ABY.<ref name="War" /> The ''Reaper'' became part of the Alignment's fleet; with it, the Alignment had the muscle it needed to keep any challengers at bay.<ref name="TEA" />

[[File:Yourfathersdaughter.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Supreme Commander Gilad Pellaeon, who assumed command of the ''Reaper''.]]
When Palpatine was reborn into a [[Cloning/Legends|clone]] body and once more took control of the Empire, Kaine joined the fight during [[Operation Shadow Hand]], during which he lost his life in [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]]. After Palpatine's [[Battle of Onderon (Galactic Civil War)|final death]] in [[11 ABY/Legends|11 ABY]], the ''Reaper'' remained with the Alignment until it was annexed into the reconstituted [[Imperial Remnant]] in [[12 ABY/Legends|12 ABY]] by then-[[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Gilad Pellaeon/Legends|Gilad Pellaeon]]. Pellaeon, having lost his flagship [[Chimaera/Legends|''Chimaera'']] during Operation Shadow Hand, took personal command of the ''Reaper'' and made it his new flagship.<ref name="The Essential Chronology" /> Pellaeon was confident that the ''Reaper'', combined with the [[Star Dreadnought/Legends|dreadnoughts]] [[Dominion (Star Dreadnought)|''Dominion'']] and ''[[Megador]]'', would be effective in defending the empire from the New Republic; after Pellaeon pitched this idea to the [[Moff/Legends|Moffs]] of the Imperial Rim factions, they agreed and made the captain of the ''Reaper'' their [[Supreme Commander]].<ref name="War" /> [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] historian [[Voren Na'al/Legends|Voren Na'al]] considered the vessel to be Pellaeon's most impressive acquisition.<ref name="The Essential Chronology" />

===Triumph and downfall===
{{Quote|The New Republic pushed Pellaeon back, but suffered a grave defeat in the Battle of Orinda. There, the ''Reaper'' destroyed most of Antilles's starfighters by annihilating the ﬂeet carrier ''Endurance''.|Voren Na'al, about the Orinda campaign|<ref name="The New Essential Chronology" />}}
[[File:LusankyavsReaper-EGW.jpg|thumb|right|330px|The ''Reaper'' faced off against the ''Lusankya'' at Orinda.]]
Pellaeon used the ''Reaper'' throughout his [[Orinda campaign|campaign]] of<ref name="BTS">''[[Before the Storm]]''</ref> 12.5 ABY<ref name="The Essential Chronology" /> to great effectiveness, [[Raid on Orinda|taking]] the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Orinda/Legends|Orinda]] and six neighboring [[Star system/Legends|star systems]] after arriving in the [[Borosk system/Legends|Borosk system]] to pick up a complement of [[Starfighter/Legends|fighters]] from the [[181st Imperial Fighter Wing/Legends|181st Imperial Fighter Wing]]. The New Republic launched a counter-assault, headed by [[General/Legends|General]] [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]] and the [[Super Star Destroyer/Legends|Super Star Destroyer]] ''Lusankya''. After the ''Reaper'' participated in battles in the [[Battle of Urce|Urce]] and [[Battle of Corthenia|Corthenia]] star systems, the massive Super Star Destroyers stalked each other around Orinda, with the ''Reaper'' and ''Lusankya'' engaging at [[Battle of Darkon|Darkon]] and [[Battle of the Traval-Pacor system|Traval-Pacor]], both battles resulting in New Republic victories. The campaign culminated in the [[Battle of Orinda]], during which the ''Reaper'', with the aid of the ''Dominion'', secured a major victory for the Empire by destroying the [[Endurance-class fleet carrier|carrier]] [[Endurance (Endurance-class)|''Endurance'']], along with its entire complement of starfighters—as its [[Commander/Legends|commander]] had decided to hold them back to protect them from Imperial fire. The New Republic and ''Lusankya'' fled Orinda, with the starfighter group [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] covering their retreat.<ref name="BTS" />

Within the following year, the planet [[Adumar/Legends|Adumar]] joined the New Republic. From the ''Reaper'', Pellaeon led a [[Orbital bombardment/Legends|bombardment]] of the world, which turned into a [[Campaign against Adumar|protracted campaign]]. While he was preoccupied, two full New Republic fleets captured the [[Antemeridian sector/Legends|Antemeridian sector]] and much of its surrounding [[Realspace/Legends|space]] from the Imperial Moff [[Tol Getelles]], and were thus poised to strike at the heart of the Empire.<ref name="NEGTC">''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]''</ref> In alarm, the Moffs ordered Pellaeon across the [[Hydian Way/Legends|Hydian Way]] to halt the New Republic advance.<ref name="TEA" /> Pellaeon was forced to move the ''Reaper'' from Adumar, and though he inflicted severe losses on New Republic forces in the ensuing campaign, the Admiral continued to be pushed back. At [[Battle of Celanon|Celanon]] in [[13 ABY/Legends|13 ABY]], he went up against the New Republic once more in the ''Reaper''. Though the fight ultimately saved the Empire from destruction, the New Republic emerged victorious at Celanon, and the ''Reaper'' was destroyed.<ref name="The Essential Chronology" />

==Commanders and crew==
Ardus Kaine, commander of the ''Reaper'' at the [[Time/Legends|time]] of [[Endor/Legends|Endor]], felt his assignment to the Super Star Destroyer and Scourge Squadron was a betrayal by the Emperor, as he believed that he was being pushed away to fight Rebels rather than being used as an aide. After Palpatine's death, Kaine saw himself becoming a new Emperor; to this end, he used the ''Reaper'' as the site of important political negotiations and as a key piece of his military.<ref name="TEA" /><ref name="TPA" /> With Kaine's death, Gilad Pellaeon took over stewardship of the ''Reaper'', using it as the centerpiece of his own attempt to revitalize the Empire. He was ultimately no more successful, seeing both victories and losses before the eventual destruction of the vessel under his command.<ref name="The Essential Chronology" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:SWAJ3.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The ''Reaper'' was introduced in ''Star Wars Adventure Journal'' 3.]]
The ''Reaper'' was introduced in the article "[[The Pentastar Alignment]]," written by [[Tony Russo|Anthony P. Russo]] and published in the [[August]] [[1994]] [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 3|third issue]] of [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars Adventure Journal]]'' magazine.<ref name="TPA" /> [[1996|Two years later]], in [[Michael P. Kube-McDowell]]'s novel ''[[Before the Storm]]'', an unidentified Super Star Destroyer is mentioned as playing an important part in the Battle of Orinda.<ref name="BTS" /> [[The Essential Chronology (real-world book)|''The Essential Chronology'']] of [[2000]], authored by [[Kevin J. Anderson]] and [[Daniel Wallace]], merged these two references together, identifying the unnamed ship as the ''Reaper''.<ref name="The Essential Chronology" />

The ''Reaper'' was first pictured in [[2009]]'s ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'', which was written by Wallace and [[Jason Fry]] and illustrated by [[Ian Fullwood]], [[Modi]], [[Chris Reiff]], and [[Chris Trevas]].<ref name="TEA" /> While the [[2007]] [[Starships of the Galaxy (Saga Edition)|Saga Edition]] of the sourcebook ''[[Starships of the Galaxy]]'' of [[Wizards of the Coast]]'s ''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game]]'' states that the<ref name="SotG">[[Starships of the Galaxy (Saga Edition)|''Starships of the Galaxy'', Saga Edition]]</ref> ''Executor''-class Star Dreadnoughts<ref name="War" /> ''Lusankya'' and ''Executor'' were constructed at the same time,<ref name="SotG" /> [[2012]]'s ''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' by Fry and [[Paul R. Urquhart]] states that the ''Reaper'' was the second ''Executor''-class vessel constructed.<ref name="War" /> As the ''Lusankya'' was established to be constructed in secret<ref name="SotG" /> this article assumes that the ''Reaper'' was the third vessel overall but the second publicly known vessel.
